registration_intent.domain_class: catalytic RNA / ribozyme / RNA-mediated chemical catalysis
registration_intent.rationale: HEXA-WEAVE registers write-side multi-strand molecular design composition (P up to 10^4 strand bundle, primary constraint Landauer x NP-search ceiling, primary oracle AlphaFold-class fold inference). HEXA-NANOBOT registers single molecular nano-machine architecture (10^0-10^2 atoms per actuator, primary constraint Brownian thermal floor at 310K, primary oracle molecular-dynamics simulation). HEXA-RIBOZYME registers a third orthogonal sub-axis covering catalytic RNA architecture (single ribozyme active site ~12-30 nt catalytic core, primary constraint diffusion-limit ceiling k_cat / K_M ~ 10^8 M^-1 s^-1, primary oracle chemical-kinetics simulation + RNA-secondary-structure prediction). The three-way split is genus-level: WEAVE composes strands; NANOBOT actuates atoms; RIBOZYME catalyses bonds. Cech 1982 group-I intron, Guerrier-Takada 1983 RNase P, Symons 1981 hammerhead, Wu-Lai 1989 HDV, Buzayan 1986 hairpin, Steitz 1993 two-metal-ion mechanism, and Nissen-Steitz 2000 ribosomal PTC define the catalytic-RNA literature corpus. Distinct from AlphaFold-class read-side prediction, from IsoDDE drug-design, from DNA-origami nano-machines, and from CRISPR-Cas RNP gene editing.

n6_invariant_mapping.tau_6_eq_4: 4 reaction states per ribozyme catalytic cycle (substrate-bound / transition-state / cleaved / product-released) — chemical-kinetic quartet matching tau(6) = 4
n6_invariant_mapping.sigma_6_eq_12: 12 conserved catalytic-core nucleotides — hammerhead minimal type-II+III ~13 nt (Symons 1981) / HDV antigenomic ~12 nt (Wu-Lai 1989) / hairpin A-loop+B-loop ~12 nt (Buzayan 1986); STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE not exact — corpus span 10-30 nt; raw 91 C3 honest tag
n6_invariant_mapping.J_2_eq_24: 24-element trigonal-bipyramidal phosphate transition-state pose-equivalence group — octahedral rotation group order 24 covers the 5-coordinate phosphorus TS pose-classes (Steitz 1993 two-metal-ion mechanism)
n6_invariant_mapping.phi_6_eq_2: 2 binary cleavage outcome states — phosphodiester cleaved/intact, cis-acting/trans-acting; the phi(6) = 2 dichotomy at the catalytic output

hexa_weave.object_scale: single ribozyme active site (10^2-10^3 atoms) vs HEXA-WEAVE proteome bundle (10^7-10^9 atoms total)
hexa_weave.primary_quantity: catalytic rate enhancement k_cat / k_uncat (chemical) vs HEXA-WEAVE design composition cost (J per inverse-search bit)
hexa_weave.primary_constraint: diffusion-limit ceiling k_cat / K_M <= 10^8-10^9 M^-1 s^-1 vs HEXA-WEAVE Landauer x NP-search ceiling
hexa_weave.primary_oracle: chemical-kinetics simulation (Eyring TS theory + Mfold/RNAfold) vs HEXA-WEAVE AlphaFold-class fold oracle

hexa_nanobot.object_scale: single ribozyme active site (~12-30 nt catalytic core) vs HEXA-NANOBOT single nano-machine (10^0-10^2 atoms per actuator)
hexa_nanobot.primary_quantity: catalytic rate enhancement k_cat (chemical) vs HEXA-NANOBOT mechanical work output (J per cycle)
hexa_nanobot.primary_constraint: diffusion-limit ceiling (chemical) vs HEXA-NANOBOT Brownian thermal floor (mechanical)
hexa_nanobot.primary_oracle: chemical-kinetics simulation vs HEXA-NANOBOT molecular-dynamics simulation
